courses.title: Vertex AI Fundamentals
courses.description: Introduction to Google Cloud's Vertex AI platform
courses.content: Vertex AI is a fully-managed machine learning platform on Google Cloud. It unified AutoML and AI Platform into a single environment. It allows data scientists and ML engineers to train, host, and manage ML models efficiently. Key components include Vertex AI Studio, Model Registry, and Endpoints.

key_facts.definition: Fully-managed ML platform
Combines AutoML and AI Platform

courses.title: Vertex AI Studio
courses.description: Exploring generative AI models using Vertex AI Studio
courses.content: Vertex AI Studio allows users to interact with, tune, and deploy foundation models. It provides a UI for prompt engineering (Language, Vision, and Speech). You can test different prompts, adjust parameters like temperature and max output tokens, and compare model responses before integrating them into applications.

key_facts.definition: UI for testing foundation models
Controls the randomness of model output

courses.title: Model Training & Tuning
courses.description: Training custom models and tuning foundation models
courses.content: Vertex AI supports custom training using pre-built containers or custom Docker images. For foundation models, parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT) and Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) are supported. Training pipelines can be orchestrated using Vertex AI Pipelines.

key_facts.definition: Train models with own code and containers
Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning for foundation models

courses.title: Gemini API in Vertex AI
courses.content: The Gemini API in Vertex AI provides access to Google's most capable multimodal models. It supports text, image, video, and audio inputs. Key models include Gemini 1.5 Pro (for complex reasoning and large contexts) and Gemini 1.5 Flash (optimized for speed and efficiency).

key_facts.definition: Model for complex reasoning tasks
Supports text, image, video, and audio inputs

courses.title: Vertex AI Vector Search
courses.content: Vertex AI Vector Search (formerly Matching Engine) is a high-scale, low-latency vector database. It enables similarity search using vector embeddings. It's crucial for building Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) applications, recommendation systems, and image search engines.

key_facts.definition: High-scale vector database for similarity search
Numerical representations of data used for semantic matching

courses.title: Vertex AI Model Registry
courses.content: The Model Registry acts as a central repository for ML models. It supports versioning, aliasing (e.g., 'default', 'production'), and metadata tracking. Models trained in Vertex AI, BigQuery ML, or external platforms can be registered here before deployment.

key_facts.definition: Central repository for managing model versions
Assigning tags like 'production' to specific model versions

courses.title: Model Deployment & Endpoints
courses.content: To get real-time predictions, models are deployed to Vertex AI Endpoints. Endpoints manage compute resources, auto-scaling, and traffic splitting (useful for A/B testing or canary deployments). Each endpoint exposes a REST API for client applications to call.

key_facts.definition: Infrastructure for serving online predictions
Routing traffic between different model versions on the same endpoint

courses.title: Vertex AI Feature Store
courses.content: Vertex AI Feature Store provides a centralized repository for organizing, storing, and serving ML features. It helps prevent training-serving skew by ensuring the same feature values are used during model training and online prediction. It supports both batch and online serving.

key_facts.definition: Centralized repository for ML features
Inconsistency prevented by using a feature store

courses.title: Vertex AI Pipelines
courses.content: Vertex AI Pipelines helps you automate and monitor ML systems. It allows you to orchestrate workflows defined using Kubeflow Pipelines (KFP) or TensorFlow Extended (TFX) SDKs. This is essential for building reproducible MLOps processes, from data extraction to model deployment.

key_facts.definition: Serverless orchestration of ML workflows
Kubeflow Pipelines SDK, commonly used to define pipelines

courses.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
courses.content: RAG is a technique to improve LLM accuracy by retrieving relevant information from an external knowledge base (like Vector Search) and providing it to the model in the prompt. Vertex AI supports Grounding, allowing models to cite sources and reducing hallucinations. Grounding can connect to Google Search or custom data stores.

key_facts.definition: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for improving LLM accuracy
Connecting LLMs to verifiable information sources
